{"description":"## Bug

In `internal/cmd/handoff.go`, `buildRestartCommand()` at line 541 calls:

```go
runtimeCmd = config.GetRuntimeCommandWithPrompt(rigPath, beacon)
```

This uses `ResolveAgentConfig()` which resolves the **default** agent, ignoring the `role_agents` map in town/rig settings. So when a witness (configured as `claude-haiku` via `role_agents.witness`) does a self-handoff, it restarts as the default agent (opus) instead of haiku.

## Expected

Handoff should preserve the role-based agent config. The witness manager's `buildWitnessStartCommand()` correctly uses `BuildAgentStartupCommandWithAgentOverride()` which calls `ResolveRoleAgentConfig()` — the handoff path should do the same.

## Fix

In `buildRestartCommand()`, replace the call to `GetRuntimeCommandWithPrompt()` with role-aware resolution, e.g.:

```go
simpleRole := config.ExtractSimpleRole(gtRole)
runtimeCmd, err = config.BuildAgentStartupCommand(simpleRole, identity.Rig, townRoot, rigPath, beacon)
```

This ensures handoff respects `role_agents` from `settings/config.json`.

## Key files
- `internal/cmd/handoff.go` line ~541 (buildRestartCommand)
- `internal/config/loader.go` (ResolveRoleAgentConfig vs ResolveAgentConfig)
- `internal/witness/manager.go` line ~234 (correct pattern to follow)
- `~/gt/settings/config.json` (role_agents config)

Create a PR with the fix."}

{"description":"When a witness completes a patrol cycle, gt patrol report closes the current wisp and creates a new one. But the wisps.hook_bead field on the agent wisp is set during attach and never cleared by gt unsling or gt mol detach. When a wisp is reaped (gc'd), the witness's hook still points to the reaped wisp ID. On next patrol cycle, the witness tries to bd show the reaped wisp, gets 'not found', and enters an escalation loop.

Fix: gt unsling (or wherever the patrol cycle closes/transitions) should clear the hook_bead field on the agent's wisp row. Also: gt prime/gt hook should handle 'hooked wisp not found' gracefully instead of propagating the error.

Reproduction: Run witness patrol for several cycles. After wisp GC runs and reaps old wisps, the witness will get stuck trying to show reaped wisp IDs.

Related: handoff notes mention this was manually SQL-fixed 3 times. The field is legacy (intentional no-op per hq-l6mm5) but prime_session still reads it."}

new_value: {"notes":"Implemented hook_bead stale-pointer fix across unsling/patrol/prime paths.\\n\\nChanges:\\n- Added agent hook metadata helpers that set/clear both description field and slot (slot set/clear + description update).\\n- gt unsling now clears agent hook metadata when unhooking and also self-heals when no hooked bead is found.\\n- gt patrol report clears previous patrol hook metadata; autoSpawnPatrol now sets hook metadata to the newly created patrol wisp.\\n- gt prime, gt prime --state detection, and gt hook/mol status now treat hook_bead-not-found as recoverable: they clear stale pointer and fall back to assignee lookup.\\n- Added HookBead support to beads.AgentFieldUpdates so hook metadata can be updated atomically.\\n\\nValidation:\\n- go test ./internal/cmd ./internal/beads"}

{"id":"gt-bzo","title":"gt unsling does not clear wisps.hook_bead field, causing witnesses to get stuck on reaped wisps","description":"attached_molecule: gt-wisp-v9o9
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T10:42:34Z
dispatched_by: mayor

When a witness completes a patrol cycle, gt patrol report closes the current wisp and creates a new one. But the wisps.hook_bead field on the agent wisp is set during attach and never cleared by gt unsling or gt mol detach. When a wisp is reaped (gc'd), the witness's hook still points to the reaped wisp ID. On next patrol cycle, the witness tries to bd show the reaped wisp, gets 'not found', and enters an escalation loop.

Fix: gt unsling (or wherever the patrol cycle closes/transitions) should clear the hook_bead field on the agent's wisp row. Also: gt prime/gt hook should handle 'hooked wisp not found' gracefully instead of propagating the error.

Reproduction: Run witness patrol for several cycles. After wisp GC runs and reaps old wisps, the witness will get stuck trying to show reaped wisp IDs.

Related: handoff notes mention this was manually SQL-fixed 3 times. The field is legacy (intentional no-op per hq-l6mm5) but prime_session still reads it.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/polecats/furiosa","owner":"12613144+Henry-E@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-06T10:42:13Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-06T10:50:14Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T10:50:14Z","close_reason":"Closed"}
